content.text:
{{- if .Values.workflow.enabled }}
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
  labels:
    {{- include "styls-admin-ui.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  secretStoreRef:
    name: {{ .Values.workflow.secretStore | default .Values.secretStore }}
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
  target:
    name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
    creationPolicy: Owner
    template:
      data:
        WORKFLOW_API_KEY: "{{ `{{ .workflow_api_key | toString }}` }}"
  data:
    - secretKey: workflow_api_key
      remoteRef:
        key: {{ .Values.workflow.secretName }}
        property: {{ .Values.workflow.secretProperty | default "workflow_engine_api_key" }}
  refreshInterval: {{ .Values.workflow.secretRefreshInterval | default "24h" }}
{{- end }}

content.text:
## Summary of Pending Changes

**Branch:** `feat/add-workflow-support`

---

### ✅ Staged (ready to commit)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts` | **New file** — Axios client for workflow engine API with lazy initialization, request interceptor for `x-api-key` header, methods for fetching/updating jobs |
| `env/.env.dev` | Replaced placeholder `localhost:3000` with `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com` |
| `env/.env.qa` | Replaced placeholder with `https://workflow-management-qa.makeitmes.com` |
| `env/.env.prod` | Replaced placeholder with `https://workflow-management.makeitmes.com` |

---

### 📝 Unstaged (working directory)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `AGENTS.md` | **Significant update** — Added workflow engine integration docs, environment variables table, API client patterns, Helm deployment info |
| `lib/config.ts` | Removed `FIXME` comment, cleaned up workflow config |
| `styls-admin-ui/templates/deployment.yaml` | Added `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` env var injection from secret |
| `styls-admin-ui/values.yaml` | Workflow-related values added |
| `styls-admin-ui/values/values-*.yaml` | Environment-specific workflow values (dev/qa/prod) |

---

### 🆕 Untracked

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `styls-admin-ui/templates/external-secrets.yaml` | External Secrets Operator config for syncing `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` from cloud secret manager |

---

### Overall Theme
This branch implements **workflow engine integration** following the pattern from `mes-genai-ui`, including:
- Multi-environment API URLs (dev/qa/prod)
- API client with runtime API key injection
- Kubernetes secret management via External Secrets Operator
- Updated documentation
review them

diff --git a/AGENTS.md b/AGENTS.md
index 8b91edb..b92b873 100644
--- a/AGENTS.md
+++ b/AGENTS.md
@@ -23,9 +23,10 @@ Next.js 15 (App Router) admin UI for styls.ai. Uses React 19, TanStack Query v5,
 
 - `app/` — Pages and API proxy routes
 - `app/api/` — Server-side routes that proxy to external backend with auth
-- `lib/api/` — Client-side React Query hooks and types per domain (batch, product, orders, user)
+- `lib/api/` — Client-side React Query hooks and types per domain (batch, product, orders, user, workflow)
 - `lib/auth/options.ts` — NextAuth Keycloak config; JWT carries `accessToken`, `idToken`, `roles`
 - `lib/config.ts` — `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL` helpers
+- `lib/utils/environment.ts` — Environment detection helpers (`isDevelopment()`, `isQAEnvironment()`, `isProduction()`)
 - `components/` — Shared UI components built on Radix UI primitives
 
 ### Data fetching pattern
@@ -49,10 +50,75 @@ Keycloak OIDC via NextAuth. `lib/auth/options.ts` handles JWT callbacks, realm r
 
 ### Environment variables
 
-| Variable                           | Purpose                                               |
-| ---------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`         | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL` | Product API endpoint                                  |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`   | Keycloak client                                       |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`      | Keycloak server URL                                   |
-| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                  | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
+| Variable                            | Purpose                                               |
+| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`          | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL`  | Product API endpoint                                  |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` | Workflow engine API endpoint (build-time)             |
+| `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`                  | Workflow engine API key (runtime, from K8s secret)    |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`    | Keycloak client                                       |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`       | Keycloak server URL                                   |
+| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                   | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV`               | Environment identifier (development, qa, production)  |
+
+### Workflow Engine Integration
+
+Workflow engine integration follows the pattern established in mes-genai-ui:
+
+**Configuration:**
+
+- `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` — Build-time variable, baked into bundle
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` — Runtime variable, injected via Kubernetes secrets
+
+**Environment-specific URLs:**
+
+- Dev: `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com`
+- QA: `https://workflow-management-qa.makeitmes.com`
+- Prod: `https://workflow-management.makeitmes.com`
+
+**API Client (`lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts`):**
+
+- Uses Axios with lazy initialization
+- API key injected at request time via interceptor (not baked at build)
+- Header: `x-api-key`
+- Server-side proxy at `app/api/jobs/route.ts` for server-to-server calls
+
+### Multi-Environment Setup
+
+**Build-time environment selection:**
+
+```bash
+# Build script selects correct env file
+./set-environment.sh [dev|qa|prod]
+```
+
+**Environment files:**
+
+- `env/.env.dev` — Development configuration
+- `env/.env.qa` — QA configuration
+- `env/.env.prod` — Production configuration
+
+The `set-environment.sh` script copies the appropriate env file to `.env.production` during Docker build based on `BUILD_ENVIRONMENT` argument.
+
+### Helm Deployment
+
+**Chart location:** `styls-admin-ui/`
+
+**Key templates:**
+
+- `deployment.yaml` — App deployment with WORKFLOW_API_KEY from secret
+- `external-secrets.yaml` — External Secrets Operator config for WORKFLOW_API_KEY
+- `tls-external-secret.yaml` — TLS certificate management
+- `ingress.yaml` — NGINX ingress configuration
+
+**Secret Management:**
+
+- Uses External Secrets Operator to sync from cloud secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager / GCP Secret Manager)
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` synced from `workflow-engine-api-key-{env}` secret
+- Refresh interval: 24 hours
+
+**Environment values:**
+
+- `values/values-dev.yaml` — Dev overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-qa.yaml` — QA overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-prod.yaml` — Prod overrides (HPA enabled, node affinity)

content.text:
# Default values for styls-admin-ui.
# This is a YAML-formatted file.
# Declare variables to be passed into your templates.

# This will set the replicaset count more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/replicaset/
replicaCount: 1

# This sets the container image more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/
image:
  repository: nginx
  # This sets the pull policy for images.
  pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  # Overrides the image tag whose default is the chart appVersion.
  tag: ""

# This is for the secretes for pulling an image from a private repository more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/pull-image-private-registry/
imagePullSecrets: []
# This is to override the chart name.
nameOverride: ""
fullnameOverride: ""

#This section builds out the service account more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/service-accounts/
serviceAccount:
  # Specifies whether a service account should be created
  create: true
  # Automatically mount a ServiceAccount's API credentials?
  automount: true
  # Annotations to add to the service account
  annotations: {}
  # The name of the service account to use.
  # If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template
  name: ""

# This is for setting Kubernetes Annotations to a Pod.
# For more information checkout: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
podAnnotations: {}
# This is for setting Kubernetes Labels to a Pod.
# For more information checkout: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
podLabels: {}

podSecurityContext: {}
  # fsGroup: 2000

securityContext: {}
  # capabilities:
  #   drop:
  #   - ALL
  # readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
  # runAsNonRoot: true
  # runAsUser: 1000

# This is for setting up a service more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/
service:
  # This sets the service type more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types
  type: ClusterIP
  # This sets the ports more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#field-spec-ports
  port: 3000

# This block is for setting up the ingress for more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/ingress/
ingress:
  enabled: true
  className: "nginx"
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: nginx
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffer-size: "16k"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/large-client-header-buffers: "4 16k"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: 500m
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-connect-timeout: "300s"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: "300s"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-send-timeout: "300s"
    # kubernetes.io/tls-acme: "true"
  hosts:
    - host: chart-example.local
      paths:
        - path: /
          pathType: ImplementationSpecific
  tls: []
  #  - secretName: chart-example-tls
  #    hosts:
  #      - chart-example.local

resources: {}
  # We usually recommend not to specify default resources and to leave this as a conscious
  # choice for the user. This also increases chances charts run on environments with little
  # resources, such as Minikube. If you do want to specify resources, uncomment the following
  # lines, adjust them as necessary, and remove the curly braces after 'resources:'.
  # limits:
  #   cpu: 100m
  #   memory: 128Mi
  # requests:
  #   cpu: 100m
  #   memory: 128Mi

# This is to setup the liveness and readiness probes more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/configure-liveness-readiness-startup-probes/
livenessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /
    port: http
readinessProbe:
  httpGet:
    path: /
    port: http

#This section is for setting up autoscaling more information can be found here: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/autoscaling/
autoscaling:
  enabled: false
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 100
  targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 80
  # targetMemoryUtilizationPercentage: 80

# Additional volumes on the output Deployment definition.
volumes: []
# - name: foo
#   secret:
#     secretName: mysecret
#     optional: false

# Additional volumeMounts on the output Deployment definition.
volumeMounts: []
# - name: foo
#   mountPath: "/etc/foo"
#   readOnly: true

nodeSelector: {}

tolerations: []

affinity: {}

secretRef:
  gcpTlsSecretName: "makeitmes-tls"

secretStore: "gcp-store"

# Workflow engine integration configuration
workflow:
  secretName: "styls-admin-ui-secret"
  secretProperty: "workflow_engine_api_key"

content.thinking:
Now I have a good picture. Let me provide a comprehensive review:

**Issues Found:**

1. **values.yaml** - Missing `enabled: false` for workflow (the deployment checks `.Values.workflow.enabled` but the default values don't set this, which could cause issues)
2. **external-secrets.yaml** - Uses `workflow.secretStore` but defaults to `.Values.secretStore` - this is fine but the conditional might fail if `workflow` object doesn't exist

Actually, looking more closely at the diff, `values.yaml` does have:
```yaml
# Workflow engine integration configuration
workflow:
  secretName: "styls-admin-ui-secret"
  secretProperty: "workflow_engine_api_key"
```

But it's missing `enabled: false` which is used in the deployment template and external-secrets template!

Let me check the external-secrets template again to see if it also needs `enabled`.

content.text:
## Code Review

### ⚠️ Issues Found

#### 1. **Missing `enabled` flag in `values.yaml`**
The deployment template checks `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}` and `external-secrets.yaml` also uses this condition, but `values.yaml` doesn't set a default:

```yaml
# In values.yaml — MISSING:
workflow:
  enabled: false  # ← This is missing!
  secretName: "styls-admin-ui-secret"
  secretProperty: "workflow_engine_api_key"
```

**Impact:** If deployed without environment-specific values, `workflow.enabled` will be undefined (falsy), which is actually safe for the deployment but inconsistent with the explicit pattern used elsewhere.

#### 2. **Trailing whitespace in `values.yaml`**
Minor: The diff shows a whitespace-only change on an empty comment line (line 35).

### ✅ Good Practices Observed

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **API Client Pattern** | ✅ | Properly uses lazy init + request interceptor for runtime API key injection |
| **Environment URLs** | ✅ | Correct dev/qa/prod URLs configured |
| **Secret Management** | ✅ | External Secrets Operator pattern correctly implemented |
| **Build vs Runtime Config** | ✅ | `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` (build) vs `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` (runtime) correctly separated |
| **Documentation** | ✅ | AGENTS.md properly updated with architecture details |

### 💡 Suggestions

**1. Add default `enabled: false` to `values.yaml`:**

```yaml

  enabled: false
  secretName: "styls-admin-ui-secret"
  secretProperty: "workflow_engine_api_key"
```

**2. Consider adding `enabled: true` to the base `values.yaml` comment** to show it's a required override, or explicitly keep it disabled by default for safety.

**3. In `workflowApiClient.ts`**, consider adding a warning when the API key is missing (currently fails silently in interceptor):

```typescript
  config.headers["x-api-key"] = WORKFLOW_API_KEY;
} else {
  console.warn("WORKFLOW_API_KEY not set - requests may fail");
}
```

### 📝 Summary
The implementation follows the established pattern from `mes-genai-ui` correctly. The only blocking issue is the **missing `enabled` default in `values.yaml`** which could cause confusion if someone looks at base values expecting to see all configurable options.

Let me think about this:

1. The current implementation has `enabled: true` in the environment-specific values files (values-dev.yaml, values-qa.yaml, values-prod.yaml)
2. The base `values.yaml` doesn't have `enabled` at all, which means it defaults to undefined/falsy
3. The templates check `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}` before including the workflow secret

If workflow should ALWAYS be enabled, then:
- We should remove the conditional checks from templates
- Or set `enabled: true` as default in values.yaml

But the user makes a valid point - if this is a core feature that should always be there, why have a toggle?

Actually, looking at the implementation more carefully:
- The deployment.yaml only conditionally adds the WORKFLOW_API_KEY env var
- The external-secrets.yaml only conditionally creates the ExternalSecret resource

If workflow is always enabled, these conditionals are unnecessary. However, having them doesn't hurt - it provides flexibility for local development or future scenarios where someone might want to disable it.

But the user is right to question this. If it's always enabled, we should either:
1. Remove the `enabled` flag entirely and always include the resources
2. Or set `enabled: true` by default in values.yaml

The current state is inconsistent - the env values set `enabled: true`, but the base values.yaml doesn't define it at all.

Given the user's feedback that it "should always be enabled", I should:
1. Acknowledge that if it's a core feature, the conditional might be unnecessary
2. Suggest either removing the conditionals or setting a sensible default


3. I realize the deployment has a critical flaw - without the environment variable, the workflow client won't function properly. The Kubernetes setup requires the ExternalSecret to be present, or the deployment will fail to start correctly.

content.text:
You're right - if workflow engine integration is a core feature that should always be enabled, the `enabled` toggle is unnecessary complexity. Looking at the current state:

**Current behavior:**
- `values-dev.yaml`, `values-qa.yaml`, `values-prod.yaml` all explicitly set `enabled: true`
- Base `values.yaml` doesn't define `enabled` → defaults to falsy
- Templates conditionally check `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}`

**The issue:** If workflow should ALWAYS be enabled, then:
1. The conditionals in templates are unnecessary
2. Omitting the ExternalSecret or env var would break the app (the API client requires `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`)
3. There's no valid scenario where you'd want `enabled: false`

**Options:**

**Option A - Remove conditionals** (if always enabled):
- Remove `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}` from `deployment.yaml` and `external-secrets.yaml`
- Remove `enabled` from all values files

**Option B - Set default `enabled: true`** (if keeping flexibility):
- Add `enabled: true` to base `values.yaml`
- Environment files can override to `false` if ever needed

**Which approach do you prefer?** Given it seems like a core integration, I'd lean toward **Option A** (remove conditionals entirely) and always include the workflow secret/env var. This simplifies the Helm templates and makes it explicit that workflow is a required dependency.

Want me to:
1. Remove the `enabled` checks from templates?
2. Clean up the `enabled` fields from values files?
Option A

content.thinking:
The user wants to go with Option A - remove the `enabled` conditionals from templates since workflow should always be enabled. I need to:

1. Remove the `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}` conditional from `deployment.yaml`
2. Remove the `{{ if .Values.workflow.enabled }}` conditional from `external-secrets.yaml`
3. Remove `enabled: true` from `values-dev.yaml`, `values-qa.yaml`, and `values-prod.yaml`
4. Add `enabled: true` to base `values.yaml` (or just remove the conditional entirely - actually, if removing conditionals, we don't need the enabled field at all)

Wait, let me re-read. The user said "Option A - Remove conditionals". So I should:
1. Remove the conditionals from templates
2. Remove `enabled` fields from all values files (including dev/qa/prod)

edits.newText:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
  labels:
    {{- include "styls-admin-ui.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  secretStoreRef:
    name: {{ .Values.workflow.secretStore | default .Values.secretStore }}
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
  target:
    name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
    creationPolicy: Owner
    template:
      data:
        WORKFLOW_API_KEY: "{{ `{{ .workflow_api_key | toString }}` }}"
  data:
    - secretKey: workflow_api_key
      remoteRef:
        key: {{ .Values.workflow.secretName }}
        property: {{ .Values.workflow.secretProperty | default "workflow_engine_api_key" }}
  refreshInterval: {{ .Values.workflow.secretRefreshInterval | default "24h" }}

which value shoudl I config on aws secret manager for this to work on dev?

content.thinking:
The user is asking what secret they need to configure in AWS Secrets Manager for the workflow integration to work on the dev environment.

Looking at the configuration:

1. In `styls-admin-ui/values/values-dev.yaml`:
```yaml
workflow:
  secretStore: "aws-secret-store"
  secretName: "workflow-engine-api-key-dev"
```

2. In `styls-admin-ui/templates/external-secrets.yaml`:
```yaml
data:
  - secretKey: workflow_api_key
    remoteRef:
      key: {{ .Values.workflow.secretName }}
      property: {{ .Values.workflow.secretProperty | default "workflow_engine_api_key" }}
```

So on AWS Secrets Manager (dev environment), they need:
- **Secret name**: `workflow-engine-api-key-dev`
- **Secret key/property**: `workflow_engine_api_key` (this is the default, but can be overridden)
- **Secret value**: The actual API key for the workflow engine

The External Secrets Operator will sync:
- From AWS Secret: `workflow-engine-api-key-dev` → property `workflow_engine_api_key`
- To K8s Secret: `{release-name}-workflow-secrets` → key `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`

So the user needs to create in AWS Secrets Manager:
1. Secret named: `workflow-engine-api-key-dev`
2. With a key-value pair where the key is `workflow_engine_api_key` and the value is the actual API key

This is a straightforward question about the Helm/Kubernetes/AWS configuration.

content.text:
Based on your configuration, you need to create a secret in **AWS Secrets Manager** with:

**Secret name:** `workflow-engine-api-key-dev`

**Secret structure** (key-value):
```
workflow_engine_api_key = <actual-api-key-value>
```

The External Secrets Operator will sync this to your cluster as a Kubernetes secret named `{release-name}-workflow-secrets` with the key `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`.

---

**Quick verification of the chain:**

| Location | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `values-dev.yaml` → `workflow.secretName` | `workflow-engine-api-key-dev` |
| `values-dev.yaml` → `workflow.secretStore` | `aws-secret-store` |
| `external-secrets.yaml` → `remoteRef.property` | `workflow_engine_api_key` (default) |
| K8s Secret created | `{release-name}-workflow-secrets` |
| Env var injected | `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` |

---

**Need the actual API key?** You should get this from whoever manages the workflow engine deployment for dev environment. The URL is `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com` based on your env config.
The secret name should be styls-admin-ui-secret
